seoTitle: Nullo Mountain vs Number One — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Nullo Mountain and Number One. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Nullo Mountain vs Number One: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Nullo Mountain and Number One.
heading: Nullo Mountain vs Number One — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Nullo Mountain, New South Wales and Number One, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Number One has a lower overall climate risk score (41/100) compared to Nullo Mountain (43/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Nullo Mountain or Number One safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Nullo Mountain has a flood risk score of 55/100, while Number One scores 29/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Nullo Mountain scores 61/100 for bushfire risk. Number One scores 34/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Nullo Mountain or Number One?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Number One has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
